title: Mock Exam B
description: A 22 task RHCSA style mock exam emphasizing chrony, SSH hardening, user defaults, and storage administration.

tasks:
Configure networking on client with the following settings:

IP ADDRESS: 192.168.122.27
NETMASK: 255.255.255.0
GATEWAY: 192.168.122.1
DNS SERVER: 192.168.122.3
HOSTNAME: client.exam-b.lab
Add a persistent hosts entry so registry.exam-b.lab resolves to 192.168.122.3.
Configure chronyd on server so it serves time to 192.168.122.0/24 and starts automatically at boot.
Configure chronyd on client so it synchronizes only with server and starts automatically at boot.
Set the default inactive period for newly created local users to 20 days.
Create user cato421 with UID 4421, no home directory, and password cinder9.
Create user mira with a home directory and password cinder9.
Create user jonas with a home directory, password cinder9, and password aging of maximum 45 days, minimum 5 days, warning 7 days.
Configure pwquality so passwords require a minimum length of 12 and at least 3 character classes.
Allow mira to restart firewalld on client through sudo without a password prompt. Use a sudoers drop-in.
On server, configure sshd to listen on TCP port 2222 and keep password and public key authentication enabled.
On server, add a permanent rich firewall rule allowing TCP port 2222 only from 192.168.122.0/24.
As mira on client, generate an ED25519 SSH key pair with no passphrase.
On server, create user meshremote with password cinder9 if it does not already exist. Then install mira's public key for meshremote and verify passwordless SSH access on port 2222.
Use rsync over SSH port 2222 to copy /opt/exam-b/report.txt to /home/meshremote/inbox/report.txt on server.
On server, create user meshremote with password cinder9 if it does not already exist. Then install mira's public key for meshremote and verify passwordless SSH access on port 2222.
Use rsync over SSH port 2222 to copy /opt/exam-b/report.txt to /home/meshremote/inbox/report.txt on server.
On server, create user meshremote with password cinder9 if it does not already exist. Then install mira's public key for meshremote and verify passwordless SSH access on port 2222.
Use rsync over SSH port 2222 to copy /opt/exam-b/report.txt to /home/meshremote/inbox/report.txt on server.
On server, create user meshremote with password cinder9 if it does not already exist. Then install mira's public key for meshremote and verify passwordless SSH access on port 2222.
Use rsync over SSH port 2222 to copy /opt/exam-b/report.txt to /home/meshremote/inbox/report.txt on server.
On server, create user meshremote with password cinder9 if it does not already exist. Then install mira's public key for meshremote and verify passwordless SSH access on port 2222.
solution_commands: nmcli connection modify "System eth1" ipv4.addresses 192.168.122.27/24 ipv4.gateway 192.168.122.1 ipv4.dns 192.168.122.3 ipv4.method manual connection.autoconnect yes
firewall-cmd --permanent --add-rich-rule='rule family="ipv4" source address="192.168.122.0/24" port protocol="tcp" port="2222" accept'
checks: grep -Eq '^server server iburst$' /etc/chrony.conf && systemctl is-enabled chronyd | grep -qx enabled && grep -Eq '^allow 192\.168\.122\.0/24$' /etc/chrony.conf && systemctl is-enabled chronyd | grep -qx enabled

useradd -D | grep -Eq 'INACTIVE=20' && getent passwd cato421 | awk -F: '{print $3":"$6}' | grep -qx '4421:' && chage -l jonas | grep -Eq 'Maximum.*45' && grep -Eq '^minlen\s*=\s*12$' /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/coremesh.conf && grep -Eq '^minclass\s*=\s*3$' /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/coremesh.conf && grep -Eq '^mira .*NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/systemctl restart firewalld$' /etc/sudoers.d/mira-firewalld

grep -Eq '^Port 2222$' /etc/ssh/sshd_config && firewall-cmd --list-rich-rules | grep -Fq 'port port="2222" protocol="tcp" accept' && runuser -l mira -c 'ssh -p 2222 -o BatchMode=yes meshremote@server true' && test -f /home/meshremote/inbox/report.txt

swapon --show=NAME --noheadings | grep -qx '/dev/sdb1' && lvs --noheadings -o lv_name,vg_name,lv_size --units m --nosuffix | awk '$1=="reviewb" && $2=="reviewvgb" && $3>=299 && $3<=301{f=1} END{exit !f}' && tuned-adm active | grep -Eq 'virtual-guest|throughput-performance'
